top_categories.workout_context: 466 miles. Your legs will beg to quit after 120. This trains the power output you need from mile 130 onward.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 466 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
466 miles rewards fat oxidation. This volume work shifts your fuel mix so you're still burning fat at hour 5, not bonking at hour 3.
At GranGuanche Audax Road, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
49,213ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
Race-pace efforts that mimic GranGuanche Audax Road's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before January.
49,213ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
106ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
GranGuanche Audax Road's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 466 miles.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 49,213ft of climbing on mixed terrain does to your power file on race day.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of GranGuanche Audax Road. Train it until it feels boring.
Low-cadence force work for 49,213ft of climbing. The slow, grinding ascents where you can't spin—you push.

race_overlay.heat: January in Lanzarote, Canary Islands, Spain. Begin heat acclimatization 10–14 days before GranGuanche Audax Road — 20–30min sauna sessions or midday rides in full kit. Pre-load sodium 48 hours out. Target 500–750ml/hr with electrolytes on race day.
race_overlay.nutrition: Ultra-distance fueling for 466 miles: 80–100g carbs/hour from mile 1 — don’t wait until you’re hungry. Practice your exact race-day nutrition in every long training ride. Carry backup calories. 466 miles burns 8,000–12,000+ calories — you cannot replace them all, but you must try.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at GranGuanche Audax Road. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.
race_overlay.terrain: Mixed terrain at GranGuanche Audax Road requires surface adaptability. Include weekly rides on mixed terrain to build confidence.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, Endurance, and HVLI Extended to prepare you for 466 miles of mixed terrain in Lanzarote, Canary Islands, Spain.
